# sync(coc): loom 2.17.0 → 2.20.0 — coc-sync-landing + hook-output-discipline + sync-completeness

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `ba6a4ce64b98` — sync(coc): loom 2.17.0 → 2.20.0 — coc-sync-landing + hook-output-discipline + sync-completeness

**Body**:

Land loom Gate 2 distribution as PR #1 per the just-synced
coc-sync-landing.md Rule 1 (COC drift MUST land first; cross-session
carry BLOCKED). VERSION 1.0.6 → 1.0.7; loom 2.17.0 → 2.20.0.

Three new MUST-bearing rules + one SessionStart hook + audit fixtures.
Two amendments to existing rules carry the kailash-py codify proposal
through Gate 2 (status: distributed in .proposals/latest.yaml):

  - autonomous-execution.md Rule 4 trigger broadened from "code review
    or self-verification" to "any gate-level review (reviewer,
    security-reviewer, gold-standards-validator)".
  - testing.md new advisory subsection "Tier-1 Conftest Stub for
    Newly-Side-Effecting Internal Methods" — captures the autouse-
    fixture pattern that collapsed a 36-call-site sweep to 1 file in
    the kailash-kaizen 2.20.0 release cycle.

worktree-isolation.md scope demoted (priority:0/baseline →
priority:10/path-scoped) per loom 2.19.0 cap-headroom — only loaded
inside agent/command/skill authoring + worktree-named paths.

settings.json SessionStart hook chain extended with the new
coc-drift-warn.js so the next session surfaces drift loudly if the
sync ever lands as working-tree drift again.

Staged via explicit `git add .claude/` per coc-sync-landing.md Rule 2
(bulk staging BLOCKED for COC-sync PRs; preserves the docs/Mock leak
fix for PR #2).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
